event: initial provisional diagnosis of acute febrile illness
initial provisional diagnosis of undifferentiated fever
scrub typhus antigen card was positive
confirmed the diagnosis of scrub typhus
other body fluid cultures were sterile
mild fatty infiltration of the liver
treated with tablet doxycycline 100 mg twice daily
MRI of the brain was done which was without any abnormality
MRI of the cervical spine revealed no abnormality
nerve conduction velocity (NCV) showed motor sensory demyelinating polyneuropathy
cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis did not reveal albuminocytologic dissociation
the patient was started on intravenous immunoglobulin therapy 0.4 mg/kg/day for 5 days
tablet rifampicin 600 mg twice daily for meningoencephalitis was added
improvement in weakness of all four limbs
aggressive limb and chest physiotherapy was continued
discharged from the hospital with full neurological recovery
followed up regularly in the outpatient department
